message:
test(fr-conformance): cross-impl DUMP/RESTORE round-trip gate for STRING (br-frankenredis-lxda)

Add `live_redis_string_dump_restore_roundtrip_matches_upstream`
to verify MIGRATE wire compatibility for `Value::String` against
the vendored redis-server oracle in both directions:

  1. Our `store.dump_key(...)` → `RESTORE key 0 <payload>` on the
     oracle → `GET key` echoes the original bytes. Exercises
     integer-encoded strings (INT8/INT16/INT32) and raw length-
     prefixed blobs including a 200-byte highly-compressible
     string that upstream may LZF-encode on the return path.
  2. Oracle `SET` → `DUMP` → our `store.restore_key(...)` → `get`
     echoes the original bytes. Confirms our decoder accepts the
     upstream envelope + INT8/INT16/INT32/LZF string encodings.

No runtime logic changes required — `Store::dump_key`/
`restore_key` already emit and consume the upstream envelope
for strings (`encode_rdb_string` covers integer-encoded forms,
`decode_rdb_string` + `decode_lzf_rdb_string` handle the
reverse path). This test is the round-trip gate the bead asked
for, and closes the STRING slice of br-frankenredis-v8s5.

Drive-by clippy cleanup (unblocks `-D warnings` on clippy):
- `fr-persist::rdb_stream::encode_upstream_stream_listpacks3_payload`:
  swap `sort_by` for `sort_by_key`
- `fr-conformance::frame_matches_expected` scan-compare helper:
  same `sort_by` → `sort_by_key` swap
- `fr-conformance::pubsub_unsubscribe_sequence_parts` return type:
  extract `type PubsubUnsubscribeParts = (Vec<u8>, Vec<Vec<u8>>,
  Vec<i64>);` to quiet `clippy::type_complexity`
- `fr-command::CLIENT_CACHING_REQUIRES_TRACKING`: mark
  `#[allow(dead_code)]` (was already dead code with an existing
  warning; now blocked by -D warnings)

fr-runtime 275 passed; fr-command 639 passed;
fr-persist 120 passed; fr-conformance 181 passed (new test
included), 0 failed;
`cargo clippy -- -D warnings` clean across fr-conformance.

Closes br-frankenredis-lxda.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
